title: manulea bellidifolia
intro: Manulea bellidifolia, also known as the Cape beggar lily, is a flowering plant species within the family Scrophulariaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its roots, which have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Manulea bellidifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
